hypotheses.claim: The first sustained normalized-VRP cross above zero predicts positive net 60-minute one-lot short iron-condor P&L.
hypotheses.signal: first daily five-minute-median VRP cross from non-positive to positive

hypotheses.claim: Causal VRP percentile-tail crossings and crossing direction identify a short or long defined-risk volatility payoff large enough to clear execution costs.
hypotheses.signals: first daily 70/75/80/85/90/95 percentile crossing, upward and downward

hypotheses.claim: VRP percentile direction, velocity, or acceleration provides a stable confidence or leverage ordering for defined-risk condor P&L.
A different defined-risk structure or a 60/120/180-minute horizon converts VRP state into positive one-lot net P&L.
Causal IV, RV, VRP, ratio, dynamics, surface, Greek, spot, and cost features can select positive-net defined-risk trades out of sample.
A ten-percentile reversal from a causal VRP tail toward zero predicts a profitable long or short iron condor.
hypotheses.requested_mapping: top reversal to long condor; bottom reversal to short condor
hypotheses.inverse_mapping: top reversal to short condor; bottom reversal to long condor

hypotheses.claim: Previously tested VRP events become economically viable when held for the maximum observable 180-minute diagnostic horizon.

future_research_boundary: Full fixed-contract chain history is required to test horizons beyond 180 minutes or multi-session holds without non-random rolling-surface survivorship.
